questions.question: Consider a Wumpus World with a standard set of rules and initial state. Describe how you would represent the domain knowledge (the rules and initial conditions) in an expert system, and explain the benefits of this representation.

In the Wumpus World, the agent has the ability to smell the presence or absence of the Wumpus, the Pit, and the exit. Compare and contrast two different ways to represent this reasoning process in an expert system, and describe the advantages and disadvantages of each approach.

A Wumpus World expert system is designed to learn from experience by adjusting its rules and parameters based on trial and error. Explain how you would implement this learning process, and describe the challenges and opportunities associated with using machine learning in an expert system.
